# 1. Data Import and Initial Overview

library(readxl)
library(skimr)
# Import dataset
A3_Dataset_2023 <- read_excel(
  "~/Downloads/2026T2/COMM3501/Assignment/A3 Group/A3_Dataset_2023.xlsx"
)
# Initial data overview
skim(A3_Dataset_2023)

⸻

# 2. Missing Value Analysis (Raw Data)

# Check missing values across all variables
sort(
  colSums(is.na(A3_Dataset_2023)),
  decreasing = TRUE
)

⸻

# 3. Duplicate Record Check

# Check duplicated observations
sum(duplicated(A3_Dataset_2023))

⸻

# 4. Data Cleaning and Feature Engineering

library(tidyverse)
# Remove identifier variables with no analytical value
insurance_clean <- A3_Dataset_2023 %>%
  select(
    -RecommendationId,
    -RequestId,
    -LifeId,
    -ExternalRef
  )
# Replace invalid AnnualIncome values (-1) with NA
insurance_clean$AnnualIncome[
  insurance_clean$AnnualIncome < 0
] <- NA
# Create HasAlternative indicator
insurance_clean <- insurance_clean %>%
  mutate(
    HasAlternative = ifelse(
      is.na(Alternative),
      0,
      1
    )
  )

⸻

# 5. Convert Categorical Variables to Factors

insurance_clean <- insurance_clean %>%
  mutate(
    across(
      c(
        Underwriter,
        Package,
        Gender,
        SmokerStatus,
        HomeState,
        PremiumFrequency,
        Super,
        RolloverTaxRebate,
        Life,
        TPD,
        Trauma,
        IP,
        BE,
        Severity,
        SelfEmployed
      ),
      as.factor
    )
  )

⸻

# 6. Factor Level Consistency Check

# Review factor levels
lapply(
  insurance_clean %>%
    select(where(is.factor)),
  levels
)

⸻

# 7. Data Quality Analysis and Missing Value Assessment (Cleaned Data)

# Overall missing value overview
sort(
  colSums(is.na(insurance_clean)),
  decreasing = TRUE
)

# Customer Variables

customer_vars <- c(
  "AnnualIncome",
  "AgeNext",
  "Gender",
  "Occupation",
  "SmokerStatus",
  "HomeState",
  "SelfEmployed"
)
customer_missing_rows <- insurance_clean %>%
  filter(
    if_any(
      all_of(customer_vars),
      is.na
    )
  ) %>%
  nrow()
customer_missing_rows

# Product Variables

product_vars <- c(
  "Life", "TPD", "Trauma", "IP",
  "LifeCoverAmount", "TPDCoverAmount",
  "TraumaCoverAmount", "IPCoverAmount",
  "Premium", "AnnualisedPremium",
  "PremiumFrequency", "Super",
  "InsideSuperPremium", "OutsideSuperPremium",
  "IndexationRate"
)
product_missing_rows <- insurance_clean %>%
  filter(
    if_any(
      all_of(product_vars),
      is.na
    )
  ) %>%
  nrow()
product_missing_rows

# Total Missing Rows (Excluding Alternative)

total_missing_rows <- insurance_clean %>%
  select(-Alternative) %>%
  filter(
    if_any(
      everything(),
      is.na
    )
  ) %>%
  nrow()
total_missing_rows

⸻

# 8. Missing Value Summary Statistics

data.frame(
  Variable_Group = c(
    "Customer Variables",
    "Product Variables",
    "Overall Dataset"
  ),
  Missing_Rows = c(
    customer_missing_rows,
    product_missing_rows,
    total_missing_rows
  ),
  Missing_Rate = c(
    round(customer_missing_rows / nrow(insurance_clean) * 100, 3),
    round(product_missing_rows / nrow(insurance_clean) * 100, 3),
    round(total_missing_rows / nrow(insurance_clean) * 100, 3)
  ),
  Completion_Rate = c(
    round(100 - customer_missing_rows / nrow(insurance_clean) * 100, 3),
    round(100 - product_missing_rows / nrow(insurance_clean) * 100, 3),
    round(100 - total_missing_rows / nrow(insurance_clean) * 100, 3)
  )
)

⸻

# 9. Data Quality Notes

# Notes:
# • AnnualIncome contained invalid values (-1) which were recoded as NA
# • No duplicate records were identified
# • Alternative was retained and converted into HasAlternative
# • Remaining missing values are minimal and primarily concentrated in product-related variables
# • Dataset exhibits high completeness (>99.7%) and is suitable for further analysis
